title: Taiwan 8-Day Itinerary
summary: Taipei base with arrival and departure flights, one Hualien day, and one northeast coast day.

days.label: Day 1
days.focus: Arrival, skyline, easy night market stop
days.transport_note: Keep this day on the MRT or by taxi. Both stops are easy after check-in.

places.name: Taipei 101 Observatory
places.summary: Fast elevator, skyline view, and an easy first stop without extra walking.
places.transport: Take the MRT Red Line to Taipei 101/World Trade Center Station and follow the mall signs to the observatory entrance.

places.name: Raohe Night Market
places.summary: Compact night market with strong street-food lineup and a straightforward walking route.
places.transport: Take the MRT Green Line to Songshan Station, Exit 5. The market starts right beside Ciyou Temple.

foods.name: Fuzhou Black Pepper Bun
foods.description: Clay-oven bun with pork, scallion, and black pepper.
image_queries: Pepper bun Raohe Street Night Market

images.title: File:Pepper bun at Raohe Street Night Market 20070622.jpg

foods.name: Sweet Potato Balls
foods.description: Fried, hollow, and chewy snack sold by the bag.
image_queries: sweet potato balls Taiwan street food

images.title: File:2011-10-10 Street vendors making sweets at a shop in Jiufen Old Street.jpg
File:East Entrance of Raohe Street Night Market 20060118 night.jpg
File:West Entrance of Raohe Street Night Market 20080910.jpg

days.label: Day 2
days.focus: Temple, memorial hall, old street, evening district
days.transport_note: This is a clean MRT day. Start in Wanhua and work north and east by train.

places.name: Lungshan Temple
places.summary: Historic temple with carved roofs, incense, and active worship throughout the day.
places.transport: Take the MRT Blue Line to Longshan Temple Station, Exit 1. The temple is directly outside.

places.name: Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall
places.summary: Large memorial plaza with formal gardens and the hourly guard ceremony.
places.transport: Take the MRT Red or Green Line to Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall Station, Exit 5.

places.name: Dihua Street
places.summary: Historic trading street for tea, dried goods, fabric stores, and restored shophouses.
places.transport: Take the MRT to Beimen or Daqiaotou and walk into the Dadaocheng area.

foods.name: Taiwan Oolong Tea
foods.description: Good stop for tea tasting and easy gifts.
foods.where: Traditional tea shops on Dihua Street

places.name: Ximending
places.summary: Pedestrian district for snack stops, shopping, and neon-heavy night photos.
highlights: Walking streets and pop culture shops
places.transport: Take the MRT Blue or Green Line to Ximen Station and use Exit 6 for the central walking area.

foods.name: Ay-Chung Flour-Rice Noodles
foods.description: Thin vermicelli soup sold as a fast standing snack.

foods.name: Brown Sugar Boba Milk
foods.description: Fresh milk with warm brown-sugar pearls.

images.title: File:Pearl Milk Tea in Chun Shui Tang.jpg

days.label: Day 3
days.transport_note: Use a rental car or a hired driver. Lookouts and trailheads are spread out and public transport is slow.

places.name: Crocodile Island Viewpoint
places.summary: Reservoir viewpoint over the peninsula shape that gives the site its nickname.
places.transport: Drive to the roadside pull-off near the viewpoint trailhead. Final access is on foot over a short but steep path.

places.name: Thousand-Island Lake
places.summary: Series of reservoir inlets framed by tea fields and layered ridgelines.
places.transport: Continue by car from the Crocodile Island area; viewpoints are spread across the ridge roads.

places.name: Yongan Trail
places.summary: Short shaded trail through tea-country forest with reservoir views in sections.
places.transport: Reach by car from the Shiding viewpoint roads. Trail access is easiest with your own vehicle.

images.title: File:Shiding River Walking Trail 石碇溪步道 - panoramio.jpg

places.name: Shiding Old Street
places.summary: Short old street with stilted riverside buildings and tofu-focused snack shops.
places.transport: Drive back down into central Shiding and park before walking the old street.

foods.name: Tofu Ice Cream
foods.description: Cold tofu-based dessert sold by local tofu shops.

days.label: Day 4
days.transport_note: Take an early train from Taipei to Hualien, then use a pre-booked driver or day tour inside the park corridor.

places.name: Taroko National Park
places.summary: Marble gorge, river canyons, and cliff roads. Best done with same-week checks on road and trail status.
highlights: Scenic stops along the gorge corridor
places.transport: Take a morning train to Hualien, then continue by taxi, chartered car, or organized tour. Do not assume every gorge trail is open.
places.access_note: Taroko conditions can change. Official January 2026 notices show selected facilities and trails open, while damaged zones and some guided sections remain restricted after the 2024 earthquake and later storms.

foods.name: Bamboo Tube Rice
foods.description: Sticky rice steamed in bamboo, often sold around indigenous food stops.
foods.where: Roadside vendors near Taroko and Hualien

days.label: Day 5
days.transport_note: Fastest option is a driver. Public option is train to Ruifang plus local buses or taxi hops.

places.name: Yehliu Geopark
places.summary: Coastal rock park with sea-eroded formations and open ocean exposure.
places.transport: Driver or bus from Taipei to Yehliu Geopark. Enter early to avoid the mid-morning crowd.

places.name: Shifen Waterfall
places.summary: Broad waterfall reached by paved paths and bridge crossings from the visitor side.
places.transport: From Ruifang or Jiufen, continue by train or car toward Shifen. Walk from the visitor area to the falls.

images.title: File:Shifen Waterfall 十分瀑布 - panoramio.jpg

places.name: Jiufen Old Street
places.summary: Steep lantern-lined lanes, tea houses, and ocean viewpoints. Best after daylight but before the late-night bus crush.
places.transport: Reach Jiufen by bus, taxi, or driver from Ruifang or Shifen. Expect stairs and uphill lanes.

foods.name: Taro Balls
foods.description: Chewy taro and sweet potato balls served hot or cold.
foods.where: Dessert shops on Jiufen Old Street

manual_images.title: File:Taro Balls at Jiufen, New Taipei City, Sept 2011.jpg

images.title: File:Taro Balls at Jiufen, New Taipei City, Sept 2011.jpg

foods.name: Peanut Ice Cream Roll
foods.description: Thin wrapper with shaved peanut brittle, ice cream, and cilantro.

manual_images.title: File:A-Zhu Peanut Ice Cream Roll, Jiufen Old Street, 2024.jpg

images.title: File:A-Zhu Peanut Ice Cream Roll, Jiufen Old Street, 2024.jpg
File:A-Zhu Peanut Ice Cream Roll, Jiufen Old Street, 2024.jpg

days.label: Day 6
days.focus: North Taipei temples, coast, final market
days.transport_note: Mix MRT with taxi for the temple-to-coast jump. Return south for the night market.

places.name: Guandu Temple
places.summary: River-facing temple complex with tunnels, roof terraces, and long-standing Mazu worship.
places.transport: Take the MRT Red Line to Guandu Station, then bus or taxi to the temple entrance.

manual_images.title: File:Ceiling of the Guandu Yunu Temple 20241122.jpg

images.title: File:Ceiling of the Guandu Yunu Temple 20241122.jpg

places.name: Tianyuan Temple
places.summary: Circular multi-tier temple complex known for blossom season and broad open grounds.
places.transport: Taxi or local bus from Tamsui area. This stop is easier by car than by full MRT-only routing.

manual_images.title: File:Tiansheng Gate, Tamsui Wuji Tianyuan Temple 20230323.jpg
File:Tian-sheng Gate, Tamsui Wu-ji Tian-yuan Temple 20140629.jpg

images.title: File:Tiansheng Gate, Tamsui Wuji Tianyuan Temple 20230323.jpg

places.name: Baishawan Beach
places.summary: Wide sandy beach on the north coast for a final low-effort outdoor stop.
places.transport: Continue by car from Tamsui or Tianyuan. Public transport is possible but slower and more fragmented.

places.name: Shilin Night Market
places.summary: Large final-night market with heavier crowd volume and a wider snack mix than Raohe.
places.transport: Take the MRT Red Line to Jiantan Station and walk south into the market area.

foods.name: Oyster Omelet
foods.description: Egg, starch batter, oysters, and sweet-savory sauce.

foods.name: XXL Fried Chicken Cutlet
foods.description: Large flat fried cutlet with pepper and chili seasoning.

manual_images.title: File:Hot-Star fried-chicken cutlet at Zhongli Night Market.jpg

images.title: File:Hot-Star fried-chicken cutlet at Zhongli Night Market.jpg

foods.name: Stinky Tofu
foods.description: Fermented tofu, deep-fried and served with pickles.

manual_images.title: File:Stinky tofu at the Xincheng Night Market.jpg

images.title: File:Stinky tofu at the Xincheng Night Market.jpg

gallery.alt: Fucha Graduation and Maus Birthday celebration

days.label: Day 8
days.transport_note: Plan an early airport transfer from central Taipei to TPE.
